Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responseive To Uncommongoods': 1. Inspection Demands, Set One; 2. Form Interrogatories, Set One; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-18-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jared R. Smith for defendant and moving party Uncommongoods, LLC
Laralei Paras and Keith Adams for plaintiff and opposing party: Rusell Brimer
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents set #1 is Granted.
The general objections are stricken. The Code of Civil Procedure does not authorize general objections. The remaining objections, other than based on the attorney client or attorney work product privileges, are overruled and stricken. It is insufficient to respond by describing categories of documents that may be in the possession of the propounding party. The reservation of the right to amend the response is also stricken. The response must be complete at the time it is made. If there are no responsive documents for a particular request, plaintiff must comply with CCP Section 2031.230. Lastly, it is not a proper response to state generally that responsive documents will be produced at the appropriate time as set forth in CCP Section 2034. While defendant is not entitled to disclosure of the identity or reports of any consultant at this time, any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege must be identified with particularity in a privilege log. The Court finds that plaintiff asserted unmeritorious objections and made evasive responses. However, monetary sanctions will not be imposed in light of defendant's failure to make a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the issues relating to the scheduling of depositions. Further responses consistent with this order and production of documents shall occur on or before May 5, 2006. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Defendant'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set #1
Off Calendar Apr-18-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jared R. Smith for defendant and moving party Uncommongoods, LLC
Laralei Paras and Keith Adams for plaintiff and opposing party: Rusell Brimer

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories set #1 is Granted in part.
The general objections are stricken. The Code of Civil Procedure does not authorize general objections. The remaining objections, other than based on the attorney client or attorney work product privileges, are overruled and stricken. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to defining the term 'incident'. The motion is denied as to interrogatory 12.2. The Court makes no rulings as to the sufficiency of any supplemental responses that have been served. The Court finds that plaintiff asserted unmeritorious objections and made evasive responses. However, monetary sanctions will not be imposed in light of defendant's failure to make a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the issues relating to the scheduling of depositions. Plaintiff is ordered to serve complete verified responses consistent with this order on or before May 5, 2006. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
